name: python3-dotenv
title: python3-dotenv
summary: Get and set values in the .env file in local and production servers
description:
Get and set values in the .env file in local and production servers
Reads key value pairs from the .env file and adds them to environment.
variables. It is useful to manage settings during development and in
production following the 12-factor principles: http://12factor.net/
The executable is named python-dotenv

arguments.description: Whether to write the dot file as an executable bash script.
arguments.raw: -e  Whether to write the dot file as an executable bash script.

arguments.description: Whether to write the dot file as an executable bash script.
arguments.raw: --export  Whether to write the dot file as an executable bash script.

arguments.description: Location of the .env file, defaults to .env file in current working directory.
arguments.raw: -f  Location of the .env file, defaults to .env file in current working directory.

arguments.description: Location of the .env file, defaults to .env file in current working directory.
arguments.raw: --file  Location of the .env file, defaults to .env file in current working directory.

arguments.description: Whether to quote or not the variable values. Default mode is always. This does not af-
arguments.raw: -q  Whether to quote or not the variable values. Default mode is always. This does not af-

arguments.description: Whether to quote or not the variable values. Default mode is always. This does not af-
arguments.raw: --quote  Whether to quote or not the variable values. Default mode is always. This does not af-

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python-dotenv because Get and set values in the .env file in local and production servers.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python-dotenv as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python-dotenv [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...` as documented upstream for python-dotenv.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python-dotenv with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python-dotenv --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
